intitule: Reading Comprehension: Prevention of Bush Fires and Felling of Trees

enonce.texte:
Read the magazine article carefully and answer the questions that follow.

PROTECTING OUR ENVIRONMENT

Every year, bush fires destroy thousands of hectares of forest in Cameroon. These fires are often caused by human activities such as slash-and-burn agriculture, careless cigarette disposal, and the burning of rubbish. Bush fires not only destroy trees but also kill animals and release harmful smoke into the air. The felling of trees, or deforestation, is another serious problem. People cut down trees for firewood, construction, and farming. This practice leads to soil erosion, loss of biodiversity, and climate change.

To prevent bush fires, we should avoid burning rubbish during the dry season, extinguish cigarettes properly, and create firebreaks. To reduce tree felling, we should plant more trees, use alternative sources of energy like solar power, and recycle paper and wood products. Community awareness campaigns can also help educate people about the importance of protecting our forests. We must remember that forests are the lungs of our planet. If we destroy them, we destroy our future.
images: A forest fire burning trees with smoke rising into the sky, and nearby people working to extinguish the fire with water and tools.

questions.texte: What are the main causes of bush fires mentioned in the text?
questions.reponse: Slash-and-burn agriculture, careless cigarette disposal, and the burning of rubbish.

questions.texte: List three negative effects of bush fires.
questions.reponse: Destruction of trees, killing of animals, and release of harmful smoke into the air.

questions.texte: Why do people cut down trees according to the text?
questions.reponse: People cut down trees for firewood, construction, and farming.

questions.texte: What are the consequences of deforestation?
questions.reponse: Soil erosion, loss of biodiversity, and climate change.

questions.texte: Propose three ways to prevent bush fires and tree felling, based on the text.
questions.reponse: 1. Avoid burning rubbish during the dry season. 2. Extinguish cigarettes properly. 3. Create firebreaks. / For tree felling: 1. Plant more trees. 2. Use alternative sources of energy. 3. Recycle paper and wood products.

intitule: Grammar Practice: Noun Phrases (Countable and Uncountable)

enonce.texte: Read the sentences and identify whether the underlined nouns are countable (C) or uncountable (U). Then complete the sentences with the correct quantifier: some, any, much, many, a lot of, a few, a little.

questions.texte: There is _______ smoke coming from the forest fire.
We need to plant _______ trees this year.
How _______ water is needed to extinguish the fire?
There aren't _______ forests left in this region.
She added _______ sugar to the mixture.
We saw _______ animals running away from the fire.

intitule: Writing a Paragraph on the Prevention of Bush Fires

enonce.texte: Write a short paragraph (5-7 sentences) on how to prevent bush fires in your community. Use at least three countable nouns and three uncountable nouns in your paragraph.

questions.texte: Write your paragraph here.
questions.reponse:
Sample answer:

Bush fires are a serious problem in our community. Every year, fires (countable) destroy many trees (countable) and cause pollution (uncountable). To prevent bush fires, we should educate people about the dangers of burning rubbish (uncountable). We should also avoid throwing cigarettes (countable) carelessly during the dry season. Creating firebreaks can stop fires (countable) from spreading. It is important to have enough water (uncountable) and equipment (uncountable) to fight fires quickly. Community awareness campaigns can save many lives (countable) and protect our environment.

intitule: Rearranging Sentences about Bush Fire Prevention

enonce.texte: Rearrange the following sentences to form a logical paragraph about preventing bush fires.

questions.texte:
Sentences:

A. This will help prevent fires from starting.
B. Bush fires are dangerous because they destroy forests and kill animals.
C. People should also avoid burning rubbish during the dry season.
D. There are several ways to prevent bush fires.
E. We can all play a part in protecting our environment.
questions.reponse:
B, D, C, A, E

Bush fires are dangerous because they destroy forests and kill animals. There are several ways to prevent bush fires. People should also avoid burning rubbish during the dry season. This will help prevent fires from starting. We can all play a part in protecting our environment.

enonce.texte: Classify the following nouns as countable (C) or uncountable (U).

questions.texte: tree
water
fire
smoke
animal
information

enonce.texte: Create a campaign poster to raise awareness about the prevention of bush fires and tree felling. Include a title, three tips, a slogan, and an image description. Use at least two countable and two uncountable nouns.

questions.texte: Write your poster here.
questions.reponse:
Sample answer:

🔥 PROTECT OUR FORESTS! 🔥

🌳 Stop Bush Fires and Tree Felling 🌳

Here are 3 simple tips:

  • ❌ Don't burn rubbish or rubbish (uncountable) during the dry season
  • ✅ Plant more trees (countable)
  • ✅ Use alternative sources of energy (uncountable) like solar power

🌿 SAVE OUR PLANET 🌿

Protect our forests (countable) and wildlife (uncountable) for future generations!
